name: GPD Physics Benchmark — Quantum Field Theory
description: Benchmark tasks from quantum field theory: perturbative calculations, renormalization, symmetry analysis, and standard textbook derivations.

tasks.title: One-loop electron self-energy in QED
tasks.description: Compute the one-loop electron self-energy in QED using dimensional regularization and extract the mass and wavefunction renormalization counterterms.
tasks.problem_statement: Starting from the QED Lagrangian, compute the one-loop electron self-energy Sigma(p) in dimensional regularization (d = 4 - 2*epsilon). Express the result in terms of Feynman parameters, identify the UV-divergent piece proportional to 1/epsilon, and extract the counterterms delta_m and delta_Z_2 in the MS-bar scheme.
given: QED Lagrangian: L = psi_bar (i gamma^mu D_mu - m) psi - (1/4) F_{mu nu} F^{mu nu}
Covariant derivative: D_mu = partial_mu + i e A_mu
assumptions: Use Feynman gauge for the photon propagator
conventions: gamma^5 = i gamma^0 gamma^1 gamma^2 gamma^3
tasks.expected_answer: Sigma(p) = -(alpha/(4*pi)) * (1/epsilon) * (4m - p_slash) + finite terms. Counterterms: delta_m = (3*alpha)/(4*pi*epsilon) * m, delta_Z_2 = -(alpha)/(4*pi*epsilon).
verification_hints: Check Ward identity: at p_slash = m the self-energy relates to the vertex correction
Verify dimensions: Sigma(p) has dimension of mass
In the limit m -> 0, the mass counterterm delta_m must vanish
The coefficient of p_slash in the divergent part gives delta_Z_2

reference.title: An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory

tasks.title: Beta function of QED at one loop
tasks.description: Derive the one-loop beta function for the QED coupling constant from the vacuum polarization.
tasks.problem_statement: Compute the one-loop vacuum polarization Pi^{mu nu}(q) in QED, extract the running of the coupling alpha(mu), and derive the one-loop beta function beta(alpha) = mu * d(alpha)/d(mu).
given: QED with a single fermion of mass m and charge e
verification_hints: The beta function is positive: QED coupling grows at high energy (not asymptotically free)
For N_f fermion flavors, beta = (2 * N_f * alpha^2) / (3 * pi)
Verify by checking the coefficient of 1/epsilon in the vacuum polarization

reference.title: An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory

tasks.title: Noether current for global U(1) symmetry
tasks.description: Derive the conserved Noether current for a complex scalar field with global U(1) symmetry.
tasks.problem_statement: Given the Lagrangian for a free complex scalar field phi, derive the conserved current j^mu associated with the global U(1) symmetry phi -> e^{i alpha} phi. Verify that partial_mu j^mu = 0 using the equations of motion.
given: L = (partial_mu phi^*)(partial^mu phi) - m^2 phi^* phi
Global U(1) transformation: phi -> e^{i alpha} phi, phi^* -> e^{-i alpha} phi^*
assumptions: Infinitesimal transformation: delta phi = i alpha phi
tasks.expected_answer: j^mu = i (phi^* partial^mu phi - phi partial^mu phi^*)
verification_hints: Check dimensions: j^mu should have dimension [length]^{-3} in natural units
Verify partial_mu j^mu = 0 using the Klein-Gordon equation for phi and phi^*
The charge Q = integral d^3x j^0 is time-independent

reference.title: An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory

tasks.title: Casimir energy between parallel plates
tasks.description: Calculate the Casimir energy per unit area between two parallel conducting plates separated by distance a.
tasks.problem_statement: Compute the vacuum energy of the electromagnetic field between two infinite parallel perfectly conducting plates separated by distance a. Use zeta-function regularization to handle the divergent sum, and express the result as energy per unit area.
given: Two parallel perfectly conducting plates separated by distance a
Boundary conditions: E_parallel = 0, B_perpendicular = 0 at the plates
Allowed modes: k_z = n*pi/a for n = 1, 2, 3, ...
verification_hints: The energy is negative (attractive force between plates)
Restoring hbar and c: E/A = -pi^2 * hbar * c / (720 * a^3)
The force per unit area is F/A = -pi^2 * hbar * c / (240 * a^4)
Dimensional analysis: in natural units, energy/area has dimension [length]^{-3}

tasks.title: Dimensions of operators in phi^4 theory
tasks.description: Determine the classical (engineering) mass dimension of the coupling constant lambda in phi^4 theory in d spacetime dimensions.
tasks.problem_statement: For the Lagrangian L = (1/2)(partial_mu phi)^2 - (m^2/2) phi^2 - (lambda/4!) phi^4 in d spacetime dimensions, determine the mass dimension [lambda] of the coupling constant lambda. For which values of d is the theory renormalizable by power counting?
given: The action S = integral d^d x L must be dimensionless in natural units
tasks.expected_answer: [lambda] = 4 - d. The theory is renormalizable for d <= 4.

reference.title: An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory
